unverified.label: Aviation-Style Safety Standards
unverified.quote: With aircrafts, there has to be an airworthiness certificate before the airplane can be sold. With medicines... the medicine has to go through extensive clinical trials before it is allowed to be sold.

unverified.label: Propagation of Civilization via Human Minds
unverified.quote: we've always propagated civilization through human minds and we've spent about a trillion person years doing that... and now we're you know we're danger of throwing that away

unverified.label: Strict and Enforceable AI Regulation
unverified.quote: Eventually, you have to have a regulation, saying that to be sold, to be certified to be connected to the internet, (AI) has to pass these tests... it has to come with a proof that it doesn’t get out of control.

unverified.label: AI as Rational Agents
unverified.quote: The study of AI as rational agent design therefore has two advantages. First, it is more general than the “laws of thought” approach... Second, it is more amenable to scientific development.

unverified.label: Avoid Humanoid Deception
unverified.quote: we should avoid completely humanoid AI systems at all costs... to the extent that systems pretend to be human they are lying to us

unverified.label: Red Line Regulation & High-Risk Governance
unverified.quote: We should say here is the acceptable level and before you can turn on your product... you have to show just as all these other technologies have to show that the risk is below the level that we would be willing to accept.

unverified.label: The Untested Airplane / Runaway Bus

unverified.label: Bias as Objective Misspecification
unverified.quote: One can interpret that [bias in machine learning] as coming from again an incorrect specification of the objective.

unverified.quote: The study of AI as rational agent design therefore has two advantages. First, it is more general than the “laws of thought” approach... Second, it is more amenable to scientific development than approaches based on human behavior.

unverified.label: The Jeeves and Bertie Wooster Relationship
unverified.quote: A model for successful human-AI coexistence where the AI (Jeeves) is vastly more intelligent than the human (Bertie) but remains entirely subservient and operates in the background.

unverified.label: The Off-Switch Game
unverified.quote: If you give a robot a fixed objective even something as simple as fetch the coffee... it's simply going to disable its own off switch as one of the first things it does.

unverified.label: Fixed objectives disable off-switches
unverified.quote: If you give a robot a fixed objective, it will disable its own off-switch.

unverified.label: AI systems are grown, not designed
unverified.quote: AI systems are not designed, they are grown.

unverified.quote: Draw behavioral red lines that must not be crossed.

unverified.quote: If you are building a machine to interact with humans, ensure it self-identifies as a machine.

all_results.label: Uncertainty in Objectives
all_results.quote: I propose a new model for AI development in which the machine’s uncertainty about the true objective leads to qualitatively new modes of behavior that are more robust, controllable, and deferential.

all_results.label: Embrace Uncertainty in Objectives
all_results.quote: the machines do not know what those preferences are and this this kind of humility is crucial because if the Machine ever believes that it knows exactly what the objective is then nothing you can do will divert it from the course of action that it has chosen

all_results.label: Burden of Proof on Developers
all_results.quote: The key point here is that the onus of proof is on developers, not regulators, and the proof leads to high-confidence statements based on assumptions that can be checked and refined.

all_results.label: Provably Safe and Controllable AI
all_results.quote: we either build provably safe and controllable AI where we have absolute cast iron mathematical guarantee of safety or we have no AI at all

all_results.label: Realization of Human Preferences
all_results.quote: The principle says that the machine’s only purpose is the realization of human preferences.

all_results.label: Safety by Design (Not Post-Hoc)
all_results.quote: Instead, we need to make safe AI. Safety should be built in by design.

all_results.label: Satisfy Human Preferences
all_results.quote: The AI system should not pursue its objectives period. It should pursue the interests of human beings. It may not know what they are.

all_results.label: The Control Problem Imperative
all_results.quote: The problem of control: how do we maintain power, forever, over entities that will eventually become more powerful than us?

all_results.label: Aviation-Style Safety Standards
all_results.quote: With aircrafts, there has to be an airworthiness certificate before the airplane can be sold. With medicines... the medicine has to go through extensive clinical trials before it is allowed to be sold.

all_results.label: Ban Autonomous Weapons
all_results.quote: If you want a computer to do something a million times it's not much harder than doing it once you just write for i equals one to a million do it.

all_results.label: Mandatory Machine Self-Identification
all_results.quote: I can't see a real justification for why we shouldn't insist that machines self-identify as machines.

all_results.label: No Strict Loyalty to Individuals
all_results.quote: It's essential right that you don't have systems that are loyal to an individual because then you can end up ignoring the negative effects uh that people's actions have on others.

all_results.label: Propagation of Civilization via Human Minds
all_results.quote: we've always propagated civilization through human minds and we've spent about a trillion person years doing that... and now we're you know we're danger of throwing that away

all_results.label: Strict and Enforceable AI Regulation
all_results.quote: Eventually, you have to have a regulation, saying that to be sold, to be certified to be connected to the internet, (AI) has to pass these tests... it has to come with a proof that it doesn’t get out of control.

all_results.label: The Sole Objective is Human Preferences
all_results.quote: We can build AI systems whose only purpose is to serve the interests of human beings, of all human beings, although they and we may be uncertain about what those interests truly are.

all_results.label: AI as Rational Agents
all_results.quote: The study of AI as rational agent design therefore has two advantages. First, it is more general than the “laws of thought” approach... Second, it is more amenable to scientific development.

all_results.label: Autonomous Weapons as Scalable WMDs
all_results.quote: The endpoint, I believe, would be that autonomous weapons become cheap, selective, scalable weapons of mass destruction (WMDs).

all_results.label: Avoid Humanoid Deception
all_results.quote: we should avoid completely humanoid AI systems at all costs... to the extent that systems pretend to be human they are lying to us

all_results.label: Capabilities Require Understanding
all_results.quote: capabilities without understanding are really of no use to us

all_results.label: Game-Theoretic Interaction in the Real World
all_results.quote: you can't think of an artificial intelligence system as a thing that responds to the world always you have to realize that it's an agent that others will respond to as well

all_results.label: Human Behavior as Evidence
all_results.quote: Human preferences are what produce human behavior right all the choice behavior that we exhibit.

all_results.label: No Incentive to Modify Preferences
all_results.quote: It’s important to design them so they have no incentive to deliberately modify human preferences - e.g., to make them easier to satisfy.

all_results.label: No Moral Obligation for Harm
all_results.quote: No amount of goodwill to individual X can impose the moral obligation on me to help him in hurting a third person, individual Y.

all_results.label: No Tradeoff Between Safety and Innovation
all_results.quote: Now, regulation is often said to stifle innovation, but there is no tradeoff between safety and innovation. An AI system that harms human beings is simply not good AI.

all_results.label: Assistance Games (Three Principles of Beneficial AI)
all_results.quote: We can turn this into a mathematical framework that we call an assistance game so it falls within the economic framework of Game Theory.

all_results.label: Principles for Beneficial Machines (Assistance Games)
all_results.quote: According to him we need to redesign AI around 3 principles: 1. The AI system’s objective is to achieve what humans want. 2. But the system isn’t sure what we want. 3. And it figures out what we want by observing our behaviour.

all_results.label: Provably Beneficial AI Framework
all_results.quote: what is a mathematical framework for AI a a way of defining the AI problem so that no matter how well the AI system solves it we are guaranteed to be happy with the result

all_results.label: Red Line Regulation & High-Risk Governance
all_results.quote: We should say here is the acceptable level and before you can turn on your product... you have to show just as all these other technologies have to show that the risk is below the level that we would be willing to accept.

all_results.label: Meta-Reasoning for Search Trees
all_results.quote: It's a combination of how good the move appears to be and how much uncertainty there is about its value. The more uncertainty, the more it's worth thinking about because there's a higher upside

all_results.label: Move Fast and Fix Things (AI Regulation)
all_results.quote: Here are some more ways to “move fast and fix things”: First, an absolute right to know if one is interacting with a person or a machine...

all_results.label: Proof-Carrying Code for Hardware Regulation
all_results.quote: Proof carrying code means that the software object comes with a proof of whatever property you need it to have and that proof can be checked immediately and quickly by the hardware.

all_results.label: The Four Approaches to AI
all_results.quote: These definitions vary along two main dimensions. The ones on top are concerned with thought processes and reasoning, whereas the ones on the bottom address behavior.

all_results.label: The St. Petersburg Compromise
all_results.quote: A similar ban on small antipersonnel AWS could eliminate swarms as weapons of mass destruction. But it would allow the major powers to keep developing their autonomous submarines, tanks, and fighter aircraft...

all_results.label: Value of Information in AI Assistance
all_results.quote: There is a trade-off between the cost of asking and the value of knowing the answer.

all_results.label: The King Midas Problem (The Genie's Three Wishes)
all_results.quote: Whenever you think about giving a goal to a super-intelligent system, it’s very easy (as all the stories show) to find a loophole whereby achieving that goal to the greatest possible extent ends up having very undesirable side effects.

all_results.label: The King Midas Problem / The Genie's Third Wish
all_results.quote: This is why, when a genie has granted us three wishes, our third wish is always to undo the first two wishes.

all_results.label: Breeding Horses (The Black Box Analogy)
all_results.quote: We are basically breeding AI systems, giant black boxes with up to 10 trillion parameters.

all_results.label: The Chernobyl Analogy
all_results.quote: Despite all that effort, we had Chernobyl, and Chernobyl ended up wiping out the global nuclear industry.

all_results.label: The Gorilla Problem
all_results.quote: The problem of whether humans can maintain their supremacy and autonomy in a world that includes machines with substantially greater intelligence.

all_results.label: The Loophole Principle
all_results.quote: whenever we do hand wavy stuff there are loopholes and the thing about super intelligent machines is they find the loopholes

all_results.label: The Nuclear/Fusion Safety Analogy
all_results.quote: Ever since then, fusion research has had the goal of generating energy and benefiting the human race by controlling the generation of energy so that you don’t have a huge explosion.

all_results.quote: An AI system that harms human beings is simply not good AI.

all_results.label: The Guarantee of Safety
all_results.quote: We either build provably safe and controllable AI where we have absolute cast iron mathematical guarantee of safety or we have no AI at all.

all_results.label: The Kalashnikovs of Tomorrow
